this queue, we will select the queue with the minimum.\n\t\tthisQueueSeatSeconds := queueSum.TotalWorkSum\n\t\tklog.V(7).Infof(\"QS(%s): For request %#+v %#+v considering queue %d with sum: %#v and %d seats in use, nextDispatchR=%v\", qs.qCfg.Name, descr1, descr2, queueIdx, queueSum, queue.seatsInUse, queue.nextDispatchR)\n\t\tif thisQueueSeatSeconds < minQueueSeatSeconds {\n\t\t\tminQueueSeatSeconds = thisQueueSeatSeconds\n\t\t\tbestQueueIdx = queueIdx\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tif klogV := klog.V(6); klogV.Enabled() {\n\t\tchosenQueue := qs.queues[bestQueueIdx]\n\t\tklogV.Infof(\"QS(%s) at t=%s R=%v: For request %#+v %#+v chose queue %d, with sum: %#v & %d seats in use & nextDispatchR=%v\", qs.qCfg.Name, qs.clock.Now().Format(nsTimeFmt), qs.currentR, descr1, descr2, bestQueueIdx, chosenQueue.requestsWaiting.QueueSum(), chosenQueue.seatsInUse, chosenQueue.nextDispatchR)\n\t}\n\treturn bestQueueIdx\n}\n\n// rejectOrEnqueueToBoundLocked rejects or enqueues the newly arrived\n// request, which has been assigned to a queue.  If up against the\n// queue length limit and the concurrency limit then returns false.\n// Otherwise enqueues and returns true.\nfunc (qs *queueSet) rejectOrEnqueueToBoundLocked(request *request) bool {\n\tqueue := request.queue\n\tcurQueueLength := queue.requestsWaiting.Length()\n\t// rejects the newly arrived request if resource criteria not met\n\tif qs.totSeatsInUse >= qs.dCfg.ConcurrencyLimit &&\n\t\tcurQueueLength >= qs.qCfg.QueueLengthLimit {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\tqs.enqueueToBoundLocked(request)\n\treturn true\n}\n\n// enqueues a request into its queue.\nfunc (qs *queueSet) enqueueToBoundLocked(request *request) {\n\tqueue := request.queue\n\tnow := qs.clock.Now()\n\tif queue.requestsWaiting.Length() == 0 && queue.requestsExecuting.Len() == 0 {\n\t\t// the queue’s start R is set to the virtual time.\n\t\tqueue.nextDispatchR = qs.currentR\n\t\tklogV := klog.V(6)\n\t\tif klogV.Enabled() {\n\t\t\tklogV.Infof(\"QS(%s) at t=%s R=%v: initialized queue %d start R due to request %#+v %#+v\", qs.qCfg.Name, now.Format(nsTimeFmt), queue.nextDispatchR, queue.index, request.descr1, request.descr2)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\trequest.removeFromQueueLocked = queue.requestsWaiting.Enqueue(request)\n\tqs.totRequestsWaiting++\n\tqs.totSeatsWaiting += request.MaxSeats()\n\tmetrics.AddRequestsInQueues(request.ctx, qs.qCfg.Name, request.fsName, 1)\n\tmetrics.AddSeatsInQueues(request.ctx, qs.qCfg.Name, request.fsName, request.MaxSeats())\n\trequest.NoteQueued(true)\n\tqs.reqsGaugePair.RequestsWaiting.Add(1)\n\tqs.seatDemandIntegrator.Set(float64(qs.totSeatsInUse + qs.totSeatsWaiting))\n}\n\n// dispatchAsMuchAsPossibleLocked does as many dispatches as possible now.\nfunc (qs *queueSet) dispatchAsMuchAsPossibleLocked() {\n\tfor qs.totRequestsWaiting != 0 && qs.totSeatsInUse < qs.dCfg.ConcurrencyLimit && qs.dispatchLocked() {\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc (qs *queueSet) dispatchSansQueueLocked(ctx context.Context, workEstimate *fqrequest.WorkEstimate, flowDistinguisher, fsName string, descr1, descr2 interface{}) *request {\n\t// does not call metrics.SetDispatchMetrics because there is no queuing and thus no interesting virtual world\n\tnow := qs.clock.Now()\n\treq := &request{\n\t\tqs:                qs,\n\t\tfsName:            fsName,\n\t\tflowDistinguisher: flowDistinguisher,\n\t\tctx:               ctx,\n\t\tstartTime:         now,\n\t\tdecision:          qs.promiseFactory(decisionExecute, ctx, decisionCancel),\n\t\tarrivalTime:       now,\n\t\tarrivalR:          qs.currentR,\n\t\tdescr1:            descr1,\n\t\tdescr2:            descr2,\n\t\tworkEstimate:      qs.completeWorkEstimate(workEstimate),\n\t}\n\tqs.totRequestsExecuting++\n\tqs.totSeatsInUse += req.MaxSeats()\n\tqs.requestsExecutingSet = qs.requestsExecutingSet.Insert(req)\n\tmetrics.AddRequestsExecuting(ctx, qs.qCfg.Name, fsName, 1)\n\tmetrics.AddSeatConcurrencyInUse(qs.qCfg.Name, fsName, req.MaxSeats())\n\tqs.reqsGaugePair.RequestsExecuting.Add(1)\n\tqs.execSeatsGauge.Add(float64(req.MaxSeats()))\n\tqs.seatDemandIntegrator.Set(float64(qs.totSeatsInUse + qs.totSeatsWaiting))\n\tklogV := klog.V(5)\n\tif klogV.Enabled() {\n\t\tklogV.Infof(\"QS(%s) at t=%s R=%v: immediate dispatch of request %q %#+v %#+v, qs will have %d executing\", qs.qCfg.Name, now.Format(nsTimeFmt), qs.currentR, fsName, descr1, descr2, qs.totRequestsExecuting)\n\t}\n\treturn req\n}\n\n// dispatchLocked uses the Fair Queuing for Server Requests method to\n// select a queue and dispatch the oldest request in that queue.  The\n// return value indicates whether a request was dequeued; this will\n// be false when either all queues are empty or the request at the head\n// of the next queue cannot be dispatched.\nfunc (qs *queueSet) dispatchLocked() bool {\n\tqueue, request := qs.findDispatchQueueToBoundLocked()\n\tif queue == nil {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\tif request == nil { // This should never happen.  But if it does...\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\tqs.totRequestsWaiting--\n\tqs.totSeatsWaiting -= request.MaxSeats()\n\tmetrics.AddRequestsInQueues(request.ctx, qs.qCfg.Name, request.fsName, -1)\n\tmetrics.AddSeatsInQueues(request.ctx, qs.qCfg.Name, request.fsName, -request.MaxSeats())\n\trequest.NoteQueued(false)\n\tqs.reqsGaugePair.RequestsWaiting.Add(-1)\n\tdefer qs.boundNextDispatchLocked(queue)\n\tif !request.decision.Set(decisionExecute) {\n\t\tqs.seatDemandIntegrator.Set(float64(qs.totSeatsInUse + qs.totSeatsWaiting))\n\t\treturn true\n\t}\n\trequest.startTime = qs.clock.Now()\n\t// At this moment the request leaves its queue and starts\n\t// executing.  We do not recognize any interim state between\n\t// \"queued\" and \"executing\".  While that means \"executing\"\n\t// includes a little overhead from this package, this is not a\n\t// problem because other overhead is also included.\n\tqs.totRequestsExecuting++\n\tqs.totSeatsInUse += request.MaxSeats()\n\tqueue.requestsExecuting = queue.requestsExecuting.Insert(request)\n\tqueue.seatsInUse += request.MaxSeats()\n\tmetrics.AddRequestsExecuting(request.ctx, qs.qCfg.Name, request.fsName, 1)\n\tmetrics.AddSeatConcurrencyInUse(qs.qCfg.Name, request.fsName, request.MaxSeats())\n\tqs.reqsGaugePair.RequestsExecuting.Add(1)\n\tqs.execSeatsGauge.Add(float64(request.MaxSeats()))\n\tqs.seatDemandIntegrator.Set(float64(qs.totSeatsInUse + qs.totSeatsWaiting))\n\tklogV := klog.V(6)\n\tif klogV.Enabled() {\n\t\tklogV.Infof(\"QS(%s) at t=%s R=%v: dispatching request %#+v %#+v work %v from queue %d with start R %v, queue will have %d waiting & %d requests occupying %d seats, set will have %d seats occupied\",\n\t\t\tqs.qCfg.Name, request.startTime.Format(nsTimeFmt), qs.currentR, request.descr1, request.descr2,\n\t\t\trequest.workEstimate, queue.index, queue.nextDispatchR, queue.requestsWaiting.Length(), queue.requestsExecuting.Len(), queue.seatsInUse, qs.totSeatsInUse)\n\t}\n\t// When a request is dequeued for service -> qs.virtualStart += G * width\n\tif request.totalWork() > rDecrement/100 { // A single increment should never be so big\n\t\tklog.Errorf(\"QS(%s) at t=%s R=%v: dispatching request %#+v %#+v with implausibly high work %v from queue %d with start R %v\",\n\t\t\tqs.qCfg.Name, request.startTime.Format(nsTimeFmt), qs.currentR, request.descr1, request.descr2,\n\t\t\trequest.workEstimate, queue.index, queue.nextDispatchR)\n\t}\n\tqueue.nextDispatchR += request.totalWork()\n\treturn true\n}\n\n// canAccommodateSeatsLocked returns true if this queueSet has enough\n// seats available to accommodate a request with the given number of seats,\n// otherwise it returns false.\nfunc (qs *queueSet) canAccommodateSeatsLocked(seats int) bool {\n\tswitch {\n\tcase qs.qCfg.DesiredNumQueues < 0:\n\t\t// This is code for exemption from limitation\n\t\treturn true\n\tcase seats > qs.dCfg.ConcurrencyLimit:\n\t\t// we have picked the queue with the minimum virtual finish time, but\n\t\t// the number of seats this request asks for exceeds the concurrency limit.\n\t\t// TODO: this is a quick fix for now, once we have borrowing in place we will not need it\n\t\tif qs.totRequestsExecuting == 0 {\n\t\t\t// TODO: apply additional lateny associated with this request, as described in the KEP\n\t\t\treturn true\n\t\t}\n\t\t// wait for all \"currently\" executing requests in this queueSet\n\t\t// to finish before we can execute this request.\n\t\treturn false\n\tcase qs.totSeatsInUse+seats > qs.dCfg.ConcurrencyLimit:\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\treturn true\n}\n\n// findDispatchQueueToBoundLocked examines the queues in round robin order and\n// returns the first one of those for which the virtual finish time of\n// the oldest waiting request is minimal, and also returns that request.\n// Returns nils if the head of the selected queue can not be dispatched now,\n// in which case the caller does not need to follow up with`qs.boundNextDispatchLocked`.\nfunc (qs *queueSet) findDispatchQueueToBoundLocked() (*queue, *request) {\n\tminVirtualFinish := fqrequest.MaxSeatSeconds\n\tsMin := fqrequest.MaxSeatSeconds\n\tdsMin := fqrequest.MaxSeatSeconds\n\tsMax := fqrequest.MinSeatSeconds\n\tdsMax := fqrequest.MinSeatSeconds\n\tvar minQueue *queue\n\tvar minIndex int\n\tnq := len(qs.queues)\n\tfor range qs.queues {\n\t\tqs.robinIndex = (qs.robinIndex + 1) % nq\n\t\tqueue := qs.queues[qs.robinIndex]\n\t\toldestWaiting, _ := queue.requestsWaiting.Peek()\n\t\tif oldestWaiting != nil {\n\t\t\tsMin = min(sMin, queue.nextDispatchR)\n\t\t\tsMax = max(sMax, queue.nextDispatchR)\n\t\t\testimatedWorkInProgress := fqrequest.SeatsTimesDuration(float64(queue.seatsInUse), qs.estimatedServiceDuration)\n\t\t\tdsMin = min(dsMin, queue.nextDispatchR-estimatedWorkInProgress)\n\t\t\tdsMax = max(dsMax, queue.nextDispatchR-estimatedWorkInProgress)\n\t\t\tcurrentVirtualFinish := queue.nextDispatchR + oldestWaiting.totalWork()\n\t\t\tklog.V(11).InfoS(\"Considering queue to dispatch\", \"queueSet\", qs.qCfg.Name, \"queue\", qs.robinIndex, \"finishR\", currentVirtualFinish)\n\t\t\tif currentVirtualFinish < minVirtualFinish {\n\t\t\t\tminVirtualFinish = currentVirtualFinish\n\t\t\t\tminQueue = queue", "numLines": 400, "startLine": 400, "totalLines":     66→// This file contains a simple local (to the apiserver) controller
    67→// that digests API Priority and Fairness config objects (FlowSchema
    68→// and PriorityLevelConfiguration) into the data structure that the
    69→// filter uses.  At this first level of development this controller
    70→// takes the simplest possible approach: whenever notified of any
    71→// change to any config object, or when any priority level that is
    72→// undesired becomes completely unused, all the config objects are
    73→// read and processed as a whole.
    74→
    75→const (
    76→	// Borrowing among priority levels will be accomplished by periodically
    77→	// adjusting the current concurrency limits (CurrentCLs);
    78→	// borrowingAdjustmentPeriod is that period.
    79→	borrowingAdjustmentPeriod = 10 * time.Second
    80→
    81→	// The input to the seat borrowing is smoothed seat demand figures.
    82→	// This constant controls the decay rate of that smoothing,
    83→	// as described in the comment on the `seatDemandStats` field of `priorityLevelState`.
    84→	// The particular number appearing here has the property that half-life
    85→	// of that decay is 5 minutes.
    86→	// This is a very preliminary guess at a good value and is likely to be tweaked
    87→	// once we get some experience with borrowing.
    88→	seatDemandSmoothingCoefficient = 0.977
    89→)

    91→// The funcs in this package follow the naming convention that the suffix
    92→// "Locked" means the relevant mutex must be locked at the start of each
    93→// call and will be locked upon return.  For a configController, the
    94→// suffix "ReadLocked" stipulates a read lock while just "Locked"
    95→// stipulates a full lock.  Absence of either suffix means that either
    96→// (a) the lock must NOT be held at call time and will not be held
    97→// upon return or (b) locking is irrelevant.
    98→
    99→// StartFunction begins the process of handling a request.  If the
   100→// request gets queued then this function uses the given hashValue as
   101→// the source of entropy as it shuffle-shards the request into a
   102→// queue.  The descr1 and descr2 values play no role in the logic but
   103→// appear in log messages.  This method does not return until the
   104→// queuing, if any, for this request is done.  If `execute` is false
   105→// then `afterExecution` is irrelevant and the request should be
   106→// rejected.  Otherwise the request should be executed and
   107→// `afterExecution` must be called exactly once.
   108→type StartFunction func(ctx context.Context, hashValue uint64) (execute bool, afterExecution func())
   109→
   110→// RequestDigest holds necessary info from request for flow-control
   111→type RequestDigest struct {
   112→	RequestInfo *request.RequestInfo
   113→	User        user.Info
   114→}
   115→
   116→// `*configController` maintains eventual consistency with the API
   117→// objects that configure API Priority and Fairness, and provides a
   118→// procedural interface to the configured behavior.  The methods of
   119→// this type and cfgMeal follow the convention that the suffix
   120→// "Locked" means that the caller must hold the configController lock.
   121→type configController struct {
   122→	name              string // varies in tests of fighting controllers
   123→	clock             clock.PassiveClock
   124→	queueSetFactory   fq.QueueSetFactory
   125→	reqsGaugeVec      metrics.RatioedGaugeVec
   126→	execSeatsGaugeVec metrics.RatioedGaugeVec
   127→
   128→	// How this controller appears in an ObjectMeta ManagedFieldsEntry.Manager
   129→	asFieldManager string
   130→
   131→	// Given a boolean indicating whether a FlowSchema's referenced
   132→	// PriorityLevelConfig exists, return a boolean indicating whether
   133→	// the reference is dangling
   134→	foundToDangling func(bool) bool
   135→
   136→	// configQueue holds `(interface{})(0)` when the configuration
   137→	// objects need to be reprocessed.
   138→	configQueue workqueue.TypedRateLimitingInterface[int]
   139→
   140→	plLister         flowcontrollister.PriorityLevelConfigurationLister
   141→	plInformerSynced cache.InformerSynced
   142→
   143→	fsLister         flowcontrollister.FlowSchemaLister
   144→	fsInformerSynced cache.InformerSynced
   145→
   146→	flowcontrolClient flowcontrolclient.FlowcontrolV1Interface
   147→
   148→	// serverConcurrencyLimit is the limit on the server's total
   149→	// number of non-exempt requests being served at once.  This comes
   150→	// from server configuration.
   151→	serverConcurrencyLimit int
   152→
   153→	// watchTracker implements the necessary WatchTracker interface.
   154→	WatchTracker
   155→
   156→	// MaxSeatsTracker tracks the maximum seats that should be allocatable from the
   157→	// work estimator for a given priority level. This controller does not enforce
   158→	// any limits on max seats stored in this tracker, it is up to the work estimator
   159→	// to set lower/upper limits on max seats (currently min=1, max=10).
   160→	MaxSeatsTracker
   161→
   162→	// the most recent update attempts, ordered by increasing age.
   163→	// Consumer trims to keep only the last minute's worth of entries.
   164→	// The controller uses this to limit itself to at most six updates
   165→	// to a given FlowSchema in any minute.
   166→	// This may only be accessed from the one and only worker goroutine.
   167→	mostRecentUpdates []updateAttempt
   168→
   169→	// This must be locked while accessing the later fields.
   170→	// A lock for writing is needed
   171→	// for writing to any of the following:
   172→	// - the flowSchemas field
   173→	// - the slice held in the flowSchemas field
   174→	// - the priorityLevelStates field
   175→	// - the map held in the priorityLevelStates field
   176→	// - any field of a priorityLevelState held in that map
   177→	lock sync.RWMutex
   178→
   179→	// flowSchemas holds the flow schema objects, sorted by increasing
   180→	// numerical (decreasing logical) matching precedence.  Every
   181→	// FlowSchema in this slice is immutable.
   182→	flowSchemas apihelpers.FlowSchemaSequence
   183→
   184→	// priorityLevelStates maps the PriorityLevelConfiguration object
   185→	// name to the state for that level.  Every name referenced from a
   186→	// member of `flowSchemas` has an entry here.
   187→	priorityLevelStates map[string]*priorityLevelState
   188→
   189→	// nominalCLSum is the sum of the nominalCL fields in the priorityLevelState records.
   190→	// This can exceed serverConcurrencyLimit because of the deliberate rounding up
   191→	// in the computation of the nominalCL values.
   192→	// This is tracked because it is an input to the allocation adjustment algorithm.
   193→	nominalCLSum int
   194→}
   195→
   196→type updateAttempt struct {
   197→	timeUpdated  time.Time
   198→	updatedItems sets.String // FlowSchema names
   199→}
   200→
   201→// priorityLevelState holds the state specific to a priority level.
   202→type priorityLevelState struct {
   203→	// the API object or prototype prescribing this level.  Nothing
   204→	// reached through this pointer is mutable.
   205→	pl *flowcontrol.PriorityLevelConfiguration
   206→
   207→	// qsCompleter holds the QueueSetCompleter derived from `pl`
   208→	// and `queues`.
   209→	qsCompleter fq.QueueSetCompleter
   210→
   211→	// The QueueSet for this priority level.
   212→	// Never nil.
   213→	queues fq.QueueSet
   214→
   215→	// quiescing==true indicates that this priority level should be
   216→	// removed when its queues have all drained.
   217→	quiescing bool
   218→
   219→	// number of goroutines between Controller::Match and calling the
   220→	// returned StartFunction
   221→	numPending int
   222→
   223→	// Observers tracking number of requests waiting, executing
   224→	reqsGaugePair metrics.RatioedGaugePair
   225→
   226→	// Observer of number of seats occupied throughout execution
   227→	execSeatsObs metrics.RatioedGauge
   228→
   229→	// Integrator of seat demand, reset every CurrentCL adjustment period
   230→	seatDemandIntegrator fq.Integrator
   231→
   232→	// Gauge of seat demand / nominalCL
   233→	seatDemandRatioedGauge metrics.RatioedGauge
   234→
   235→	// seatDemandStats is derived from periodically examining the seatDemandIntegrator.
   236→	// The average, standard deviation, and high watermark come directly from the integrator.
   237→	// envelope = avg + stdDev.
   238→	// Periodically smoothed gets replaced with `max(envelope, A*smoothed + (1-A)*envelope)`,
   239→	// where A is seatDemandSmoothingCoefficient.
   240→	seatDemandStats seatDemandStats
   241→
   242→	// nominalCL is the nominal concurrency limit configured in the PriorityLevelConfiguration
   243→	nominalCL int
   244→
   245→	// minCL is the nominal limit less the lendable amount
   246→	minCL int
   247→
   248→	//maxCL is the nominal limit plus the amount that may be borrowed
   249→	maxCL int
   250→
   251→	// currentCL is the dynamically derived concurrency limit to impose for now
   252→	currentCL int
   253→}
   254→
   255→type seatDemandStats struct {
   256→	avg           float64
   257→	stdDev        float64
   258→	highWatermark int
   259→	smoothed      float64
   260→}
   261→
   262→func (stats *seatDemandStats) update(obs fq.IntegratorResults) {
   263→	stats.highWatermark = int(math.Round(obs.Max))
   264→	if obs.Duration <= 0 {
   265→		return
   266→	}
   267→	if math.IsNaN(obs.Deviation) {
   268→		obs.Deviation = 0
   269→	}
   270→	stats.avg = obs.Average
   271→	stats.stdDev = obs.Deviation
   272→	envelope := obs.Average + obs.Deviation
   273→	stats.smoothed = math.Max(envelope, seatDemandSmoothingCoefficient*stats.smoothed+(1-seatDemandSmoothingCoefficient)*envelope)
   274→}

   276→// NewTestableController is extra flexible to facilitate testing
   277→func newTestableController(config TestableConfig) *configController {
   278→	cfgCtlr := &configController{
   279→		name:                   config.Name,
   280→		clock:                  config.Clock,
   281→		queueSetFactory:        config.QueueSetFactory,
   282→		reqsGaugeVec:           config.ReqsGaugeVec,
   283→		execSeatsGaugeVec:      config.ExecSeatsGaugeVec,
   284→		asFieldManager:         config.AsFieldManager,
   285→		foundToDangling:        config.FoundToDangling,
   286→		serverConcurrencyLimit: config.ServerConcurrencyLimit,
   287→		flowcontrolClient:      config.FlowcontrolClient,
   288→		priorityLevelStates:    make(map[string]*priorityLevelState),
   289→		WatchTracker:           NewWatchTracker(),
   290→		MaxSeatsTracker:        NewMaxSeatsTracker(),
   291→	}
   292→	klog.V(2).Infof("NewTestableController %q with serverConcurrencyLimit=%d, name=%s, asFieldManager=%q", cfgCtlr.name, cfgCtlr.serverConcurrencyLimit, cfgCtlr.name, cfgCtlr.asFieldManager)
   293→	// Start with longish delay because conflicts will be between
   294→	// different processes, so take some time to go away.
   295→	cfgCtlr.configQueue = workqueue.NewTypedRateLimitingQueueWithConfig(
   296→		workqueue.NewTypedItemExponentialFailureRateLimiter[int](200*time.Millisecond, 8*time.Hour),
   297→		workqueue.TypedRateLimitingQueueConfig[int]{Name: "priority_and_fairness_config_queue"},
   298→	)
   299→	// ensure the data structure reflects the mandatory config
   300→	cfgCtlr.lockAndDigestConfigObjects(nil, nil)

\"k8s.io/api/flowcontrol/v1\"\n\tflowcontrolapplyconfiguration \"k8s.io/client-go/applyconfigurations/flowcontrol/v1\"\n\tflowcontrolclient \"k8s.io/client-go/kubernetes/typed/flowcontrol/v1\"\n\tflowcontrollister \"k8s.io/client-go/listers/flowcontrol/v1\"\n)\n\nconst timeFmt = \"2006-01-02T15:04:05.999\"\n\nconst (\n\t// priorityLevelMaxSeatsPercent is the percentage of the nominalCL used as max seats allocatable from work estimator\n\tpriorityLevelMaxSeatsPercent = float64(0.15)\n)\n\n// This file contains a simple local (to the apiserver) controller\n// that digests API Priority and Fairness config objects (FlowSchema\n// and PriorityLevelConfiguration) into the data structure that the\n// filter uses.  At this first level of development this controller\n// takes the simplest possible approach: whenever notified of any\n// change to any config object, or when any priority level that is\n// undesired becomes completely unused, all the config objects are\n// read and processed as a whole.\n\nconst (\n\t// Borrowing among priority levels will be accomplished by periodically\n\t// adjusting the current concurrency limits (CurrentCLs);\n\t// borrowingAdjustmentPeriod is that period.\n\tborrowingAdjustmentPeriod = 10 * time.Second\n\n\t// The input to the seat borrowing is smoothed seat demand figures.\n\t// This constant controls the decay rate of that smoothing,\n\t// as described in the comment on the `seatDemandStats` field of `priorityLevelState`.\n\t// The particular number appearing here has the property that half-life\n\t// of that decay is 5 minutes.\n\t// This is a very preliminary guess at a good value and is likely to be tweaked\n\t// once we get some experience with borrowing.\n\tseatDemandSmoothingCoefficient = 0.977\n)\n\n// The funcs in this package follow the naming convention that the suffix\n// \"Locked\" means the relevant mutex must be locked at the start of each\n// call and will be locked upon return.  For a configController, the\n// suffix \"ReadLocked\" stipulates a read lock while just \"Locked\"\n// stipulates a full lock.  Absence of either suffix means that either\n// (a) the lock must NOT be held at call time and will not be held\n// upon return or (b) locking is irrelevant.\n\n// StartFunction begins the process of handling a request.  If the\n// request gets queued then this function uses the given hashValue as\n// the source of entropy as it shuffle-shards the request into a\n// queue.  The descr1 and descr2 values play no role in the logic but\n// appear in log messages.  This method does not return until the\n// queuing, if any, for this request is done.  If `execute` is false\n// then `afterExecution` is irrelevant and the request should be\n// rejected.  Otherwise the request should be executed and\n// `afterExecution` must be called exactly once.\ntype StartFunction func(ctx context.Context, hashValue uint64) (execute bool, afterExecution func())\n\n// RequestDigest holds necessary info from request for flow-control\ntype RequestDigest struct {\n\tRequestInfo *request.RequestInfo\n\tUser        user.Info\n}\n\n// `*configController` maintains eventual consistency with the API\n// objects that configure API Priority and Fairness, and provides a\n// procedural interface to the configured behavior.  The methods of\n// this type and cfgMeal follow the convention that the suffix\n// \"Locked\" means that the caller must hold the configController lock.\ntype configController struct {\n\tname              string // varies in tests of fighting controllers\n\tclock             clock.PassiveClock\n\tqueueSetFactory   fq.QueueSetFactory\n\treqsGaugeVec      metrics.RatioedGaugeVec\n\texecSeatsGaugeVec metrics.RatioedGaugeVec\n\n\t// How this controller appears in an ObjectMeta ManagedFieldsEntry.Manager\n\tasFieldManager string\n\n\t// Given a boolean indicating whether a FlowSchema's referenced\n\t// PriorityLevelConfig exists, return a boolean indicating whether\n\t// the reference is dangling\n\tfoundToDangling func(bool) bool\n\n\t// configQueue holds `(interface{})(0)` when the configuration\n\t// objects need to be reprocessed.\n\tconfigQueue workqueue.TypedRateLimitingInterface[int]\n\n\tplLister         flowcontrollister.PriorityLevelConfigurationLister\n\tplInformerSynced cache.InformerSynced\n\n\tfsLister         flowcontrollister.FlowSchemaLister\n\tfsInformerSynced cache.InformerSynced\n\n\tflowcontrolClient flowcontrolclient.FlowcontrolV1Interface\n\n\t// serverConcurrencyLimit is the limit on the server's total\n\t// number of non-exempt requests being served at once.  This comes\n\t// from server configuration.\n\tserverConcurrencyLimit int\n\n\t// watchTracker implements the necessary WatchTracker interface.\n\tWatchTracker\n\n\t// MaxSeatsTracker tracks the maximum seats that should be allocatable from the\n\t// work estimator for a given priority level. This controller does not enforce\n\t// any limits on max seats stored in this tracker, it is up to the work estimator\n\t// to set lower/upper limits on max seats (currently min=1, max=10).\n\tMaxSeatsTracker\n\n\t// the most recent update attempts, ordered by increasing age.\n\t// Consumer trims to keep only the last minute's worth of entries.\n\t// The controller uses this to limit itself to at most six updates\n\t// to a given FlowSchema in any minute.\n\t// This may only be accessed from the one and only worker goroutine.\n\tmostRecentUpdates []updateAttempt\n\n\t// This must be locked while accessing the later fields.\n\t// A lock for writing is needed\n\t// for writing to any of the following:\n\t// - the flowSchemas field\n\t// - the slice held in the flowSchemas field\n\t// - the priorityLevelStates field\n\t// - the map held in the priorityLevelStates field\n\t// - any field of a priorityLevelState held in that map\n\tlock sync.RWMutex\n\n\t// flowSchemas holds the flow schema objects, sorted by increasing\n\t// numerical (decreasing logical) matching precedence.  Every\n\t// FlowSchema in this slice is immutable.\n\tflowSchemas apihelpers.FlowSchemaSequence\n\n\t// priorityLevelStates maps the PriorityLevelConfiguration object\n\t// name to the state for that level.  Every name referenced from a\n\t// member of `flowSchemas` has an entry here.\n\tpriorityLevelStates map[string]*priorityLevelState\n\n\t// nominalCLSum is the sum of the nominalCL fields in the priorityLevelState records.\n\t// This can exceed serverConcurrencyLimit because of the deliberate rounding up\n\t// in the computation of the nominalCL values.\n\t// This is tracked because it is an input to the allocation adjustment algorithm.\n\tnominalCLSum int\n}\n\ntype updateAttempt struct {\n\ttimeUpdated  time.Time\n\tupdatedItems sets.String // FlowSchema names\n}\n\n// priorityLevelState holds the state specific to a priority level.\ntype priorityLevelState struct {\n\t// the API object or prototype prescribing this level.  Nothing\n\t// reached through this pointer is mutable.\n\tpl *flowcontrol.PriorityLevelConfiguration\n\n\t// qsCompleter holds the QueueSetCompleter derived from `pl`\n\t// and `queues`.\n\tqsCompleter fq.QueueSetCompleter\n\n\t// The QueueSet for this priority level.\n\t// Never nil.\n\tqueues fq.QueueSet\n\n\t// quiescing==true indicates that this priority level should be\n\t// removed when its queues have all drained.\n\tquiescing bool\n\n\t// number of goroutines between Controller::Match and calling the\n\t// returned StartFunction\n\tnumPending int\n\n\t// Observers tracking number of requests waiting, executing\n\treqsGaugePair metrics.RatioedGaugePair\n\n\t// Observer of number of seats occupied throughout execution\n\texecSeatsObs metrics.RatioedGauge\n\n\t// Integrator of seat demand, reset every CurrentCL adjustment period\n\tseatDemandIntegrator fq.Integrator\n\n\t// Gauge of seat demand / nominalCL\n\tseatDemandRatioedGauge metrics.RatioedGauge\n\n\t// seatDemandStats is derived from periodically examining the seatDemandIntegrator.\n\t// The average, standard deviation, and high watermark come directly from the integrator.\n\t// envelope = avg + stdDev.\n\t// Periodically smoothed gets replaced with `max(envelope, A*smoothed + (1-A)*envelope)`,\n\t// where A is seatDemandSmoothingCoefficient.\n\tseatDemandStats seatDemandStats\n\n\t// nominalCL is the nominal concurrency limit configured in the PriorityLevelConfiguration\n\tnominalCL int\n\n\t// minCL is the nominal limit less the lendable amount\n\tminCL int\n\n\t//maxCL is the nominal limit plus the amount that may be borrowed\n\tmaxCL int\n\n\t// currentCL is the dynamically derived concurrency limit to impose for now\n\tcurrentCL int\n}\n\ntype seatDemandStats struct {\n\tavg           float64\n\tstdDev        float64\n\thighWatermark int\n\tsmoothed      float64\n}\n\nfunc (stats *seatDemandStats) update(obs fq.IntegratorResults) {\n\tstats.highWatermark = int(math.Round(obs.Max))\n\tif obs.Duration <= 0 {\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\tif math.IsNaN(obs.Deviation) {\n\t\tobs.Deviation = 0\n\t}\n\tstats.avg = obs.Average\n\tstats.stdDev = obs.Deviation\n\tenvelope := obs.Average + obs.Deviation\n\tstats.smoothed = math.Max(envelope, seatDemandSmoothingCoefficient*stats.smoothed+(1-seatDemandSmoothingCoefficient)*envelope)\n}\n\n// NewTestableController is extra flexible to facilitate testing\nfunc newTestableController(config TestableConfig) *configController {\n\tcfgCtlr := &configController{\n\t\tname:                   config.Name,\n\t\tclock:                  config.Clock,\n\t\tqueueSetFactory:        config.QueueSetFactory,\n\t\treqsGaugeVec:           config.ReqsGaugeVec,\n\t\texecSeatsGaugeVec:      config.ExecSeatsGaugeVec,\n\t\tasFieldManager:         config.AsFieldManager,\n\t\tfoundToDangling:        config.FoundToDangling,\n\t\tserverConcurrencyLimit: config.ServerConcurrencyLimit,\n\t\tflowcontrolClient:      config.FlowcontrolClient,\n\t\tpriorityLevelStates:    make(map[string]*priorityLevelState),\n\t\tWatchTracker:           NewWatchTracker(),\n\t\tMaxSeatsTracker:        NewMaxSeatsTracker(),\n\t}\n\tklog.V(2).Infof(\"NewTestableController %q with serverConcurrencyLimit=%d, name=%s, asFieldManager=%q\", cfgCtlr.name, cfgCtlr.serverConcurrencyLimit, cfgCtlr.name, cfgCtlr.asFieldManager)\n\t// Start with longish delay because conflicts will be between\n\t// different processes, so take some time to go away.\n\tcfgCtlr.configQueue = workqueue.NewTypedRateLimitingQueueWithConfig(\n\t\tworkqueue.NewTypedItemExponentialFailureRateLimiter[int](200*time.Millisecond, 8*time.Hour),\n\t\tworkqueue.TypedRateLimitingQueueConfig[int]{Name: \"priority_and_fairness_config_queue\"},\n\t)\n\t// ensure the data structure reflects the mandatory config\n\tcfgCtlr.lockAndDigestConfigObjects(nil, nil)", "numLines": 300, "startLine": 1, "totalLines": 1163}}

    16→
    17→package promise
    18→
    19→import (
    20→	"context"
    21→	"sync"
    22→)
    23→
    24→// promise implements the WriteOnce interface.
    25→type promise struct {
    26→	doneCtx context.Context
    27→	doneVal interface{}
    28→	setCh   chan struct{}
    29→	onceler sync.Once
    30→	value   interface{}
    31→}
    32→
    33→var _ WriteOnce = &promise{}
    34→
    35→// NewWriteOnce makes a new thread-safe WriteOnce.
    36→//
    37→// If `initial` is non-nil then that value is Set at creation time.
    38→//
    39→// If a `Get` is waiting soon after the channel associated with the
    40→// `doneCtx` becomes selectable (which never happens for the nil
    41→// channel) then `Set(doneVal)` effectively happens at that time.
    42→func NewWriteOnce(initial interface{}, doneCtx context.Context, doneVal interface{}) WriteOnce {
    43→	p := &promise{
    44→		doneCtx: doneCtx,
    45→		doneVal: doneVal,
    46→		setCh:   make(chan struct{}),
    47→	}
    48→	if initial != nil {
    49→		p.Set(initial)
    50→	}
    51→	return p
    52→}
    53→
    54→func (p *promise) Get() interface{} {
    55→	select {
    56→	case <-p.setCh:
    57→	case <-p.doneCtx.Done():
    58→		p.Set(p.doneVal)
    59→	}
    60→	return p.value
    61→}
    62→
    63→func (p *promise) Set(value interface{}) bool {
    64→	var ans bool
    65→	p.onceler.Do(func() {
    66→		p.value = value
    67→		close(p.setCh)
    68→		ans = true
    69→	})
    70→	return ans
    71→}
    72→

(\n\t\"context\"\n\t\"sync\"\n)\n\n// promise implements the WriteOnce interface.\ntype promise struct {\n\tdoneCtx context.Context\n\tdoneVal interface{}\n\tsetCh   chan struct{}\n\tonceler sync.Once\n\tvalue   interface{}\n}\n\nvar _ WriteOnce = &promise{}\n\n// NewWriteOnce makes a new thread-safe WriteOnce.\n//\n// If `initial` is non-nil then that value is Set at creation time.\n//\n// If a `Get` is waiting soon after the channel associated with the\n// `doneCtx` becomes selectable (which never happens for the nil\n// channel) then `Set(doneVal)` effectively happens at that time.\nfunc NewWriteOnce(initial interface{}, doneCtx context.Context, doneVal interface{}) WriteOnce {\n\tp := &promise{\n\t\tdoneCtx: doneCtx,\n\t\tdoneVal: doneVal,\n\t\tsetCh:   make(chan struct{}),\n\t}\n\tif initial != nil {\n\t\tp.Set(initial)\n\t}\n\treturn p\n}\n\nfunc (p *promise) Get() interface{} {\n\tselect {\n\tcase <-p.setCh:\n\tcase <-p.doneCtx.Done():\n\t\tp.Set(p.doneVal)\n\t}\n\treturn p.value\n}\n\nfunc (p *promise) Set(value interface{}) bool {\n\tvar ans bool\n\tp.onceler.Do(func() {\n\t\tp.value = value\n\t\tclose(p.setCh)\n\t\tans = true\n\t})\n\treturn ans\n}\n", "numLines": 72, "startLine": 1, "totalLines": 72}}

    40→// ConfigConsumerAsFieldManager is how the config consuminng

    44→// Interface defines how the API Priority and Fairness filter interacts with the underlying system.
    45→type Interface interface {
    46→	// Handle takes care of queuing and dispatching a request
    47→	// characterized by the given digest.  The given `noteFn` will be
    48→	// invoked with the results of request classification.
    49→	// The given `workEstimator` is called, if at all, after noteFn.
    50→	// `workEstimator` will be invoked only when the request
    51→	//  is classified as non 'exempt'.
    52→	// 'workEstimator', when invoked, must return the
    53→	// work parameters for the request.
    54→	// If the request is queued then `queueNoteFn` will be called twice,
    55→	// first with `true` and then with `false`; otherwise
    56→	// `queueNoteFn` will not be called at all.  If Handle decides
    57→	// that the request should be executed then `execute()` will be
    58→	// invoked once to execute the request; otherwise `execute()` will
    59→	// not be invoked.
    60→	// Handle() should never return while execute() is running, even if
    61→	// ctx is cancelled or times out.
    62→	Handle(ctx context.Context,
    63→		requestDigest RequestDigest,
    64→		noteFn func(fs *flowcontrol.FlowSchema, pl *flowcontrol.PriorityLevelConfiguration, flowDistinguisher string),
    65→		workEstimator func() fcrequest.WorkEstimate,
    66→		queueNoteFn fq.QueueNoteFn,
    67→		execFn func(),
    68→	)
    69→
    70→	// Run monitors config objects from the main apiservers and causes
    71→	// any needed changes to local behavior.  This method ceases
    72→	// activity and returns after the given channel is closed.
    73→	Run(stopCh <-chan struct{}) error
    74→
    75→	// Install installs debugging endpoints to the web-server.
    76→	Install(c *mux.PathRecorderMux)
    77→
    78→	// WatchTracker provides the WatchTracker interface.
    79→	WatchTracker
    80→
    81→	// MaxSeatsTracker is invoked from the work estimator to track max seats
    82→	// that can be occupied by a request for a priority level.
    83→	MaxSeatsTracker
    84→}
    85→
    86→// This request filter implements https://github.com/kubernetes/enhancements/blob/master/keps/sig-api-machinery/1040-priority-and-fairness/README.md
    87→
    88→// New creates a new instance to implement API priority and fairness
    89→func New(
    90→	informerFactory kubeinformers.SharedInformerFactory,
    91→	flowcontrolClient flowcontrolclient.FlowcontrolV1Interface,
    92→	serverConcurrencyLimit int,
    93→) Interface {
    94→	clk := eventclock.Real{}
    95→	return NewTestable(TestableConfig{
    96→		Name:                   "Controller",
    97→		Clock:                  clk,
    98→		AsFieldManager:         ConfigConsumerAsFieldManager,
    99→		FoundToDangling:        func(found bool) bool { return !found },
   100→		InformerFactory:        informerFactory,
   101→		FlowcontrolClient:      flowcontrolClient,
   102→		ServerConcurrencyLimit: serverConcurrencyLimit,
   103→		ReqsGaugeVec:           metrics.PriorityLevelConcurrencyGaugeVec,
   104→		ExecSeatsGaugeVec:      metrics.PriorityLevelExecutionSeatsGaugeVec,
   105→		QueueSetFactory:        fqs.NewQueueSetFactory(clk),
   106→	})
   107→}
   108→
   109→// TestableConfig carries the parameters to an implementation that is testable
   110→type TestableConfig struct {
   111→	// Name of the controller
   112→	Name string
   113→
   114→	// Clock to use in timing deliberate delays
   115→	Clock clock.PassiveClock
   116→
   117→	// AsFieldManager is the string to use in the metadata for
   118→	// server-side apply.  Normally this is
   119→	// `ConfigConsumerAsFieldManager`.  This is exposed as a parameter
   120→	// so that a test of competing controllers can supply different
   121→	// values.
   122→	AsFieldManager string
   123→
   124→	// FoundToDangling maps the boolean indicating whether a
   125→	// FlowSchema's referenced PLC exists to the boolean indicating
   126→	// that FlowSchema's status should indicate a dangling reference.
   127→	// This is a parameter so that we can write tests of what happens
   128→	// when servers disagree on that bit of Status.
   129→	FoundToDangling func(bool) bool
   130→
   131→	// InformerFactory to use in building the controller
   132→	InformerFactory kubeinformers.SharedInformerFactory
   133→
   134→	// FlowcontrolClient to use for manipulating config objects
   135→	FlowcontrolClient flowcontrolclient.FlowcontrolV1Interface
   136→
   137→	// ServerConcurrencyLimit for the controller to enforce
   138→	ServerConcurrencyLimit int
   139→
   140→	// GaugeVec for metrics about requests, broken down by phase and priority_level
   141→	ReqsGaugeVec metrics.RatioedGaugeVec
   142→
   143→	// RatioedGaugePairVec for metrics about seats occupied by all phases of execution
   144→	ExecSeatsGaugeVec metrics.RatioedGaugeVec
   145→
   146→	// QueueSetFactory for the queuing implementation
   147→	QueueSetFactory fq.QueueSetFactory
   148→}

   150→// NewTestable is extra flexible to facilitate testing
   151→func NewTestable(config TestableConfig) Interface {
   152→	return newTestableController(config)
   153→}
   154→
   155→func (cfgCtlr *configController) Handle(ctx context.Context, requestDigest RequestDigest,
   156→	noteFn func(fs *flowcontrol.FlowSchema, pl *flowcontrol.PriorityLevelConfiguration, flowDistinguisher string),
   157→	workEstimator func() fcrequest.WorkEstimate,
   158→	queueNoteFn fq.QueueNoteFn,
   159→	execFn func()) {
   160→	fs, pl, isExempt, req, startWaitingTime := cfgCtlr.startRequest(ctx, requestDigest, noteFn, workEstimator, queueNoteFn)
   161→	queued := startWaitingTime != time.Time{}
   162→	if req == nil {
   163→		if queued {
   164→			observeQueueWaitTime(ctx, pl.Name, fs.Name, strconv.FormatBool(req != nil), cfgCtlr.clock.Since(startWaitingTime))
   165→		}
   166→		klog.V(7).Infof("Handle(%#+v) => fsName=%q, distMethod=%#+v, plName=%q, isExempt=%v, reject", requestDigest, fs.Name, fs.Spec.DistinguisherMethod, pl.Name, isExempt)
   167→		return
   168→	}
   169→	klog.V(7).Infof("Handle(%#+v) => fsName=%q, distMethod=%#+v, plName=%q, isExempt=%v, queued=%v", requestDigest, fs.Name, fs.Spec.DistinguisherMethod, pl.Name, isExempt, queued)
   170→	var executed bool
   171→	idle, panicking := true, true
   172→	defer func() {
   173→		klog.V(7).Infof("Handle(%#+v) => fsName=%q, distMethod=%#+v, plName=%q, isExempt=%v, queued=%v, Finish() => panicking=%v idle=%v",
   174→			requestDigest, fs.Name, fs.Spec.DistinguisherMethod, pl.Name, isExempt, queued, panicking, idle)
   175→		if idle {
   176→			cfgCtlr.maybeReap(pl.Name)
   177→		}
   178→	}()
   179→	idle = req.Finish(func() {
   180→		if queued {
   181→			observeQueueWaitTime(ctx, pl.Name, fs.Name, strconv.FormatBool(req != nil), cfgCtlr.clock.Since(startWaitingTime))
   182→		}
   183→		metrics.AddDispatch(ctx, pl.Name, fs.Name)
   184→		fqs.OnRequestDispatched(req)
   185→		executed = true
   186→		startExecutionTime := cfgCtlr.clock.Now()
   187→		defer func() {
   188→			executionTime := cfgCtlr.clock.Since(startExecutionTime)
   189→			httplog.AddKeyValue(ctx, "apf_execution_time", executionTime)
   190→			metrics.ObserveExecutionDuration(ctx, pl.Name, fs.Name, executionTime)
   191→		}()
   192→		execFn()
   193→	})
   194→	if queued && !executed {
   195→		observeQueueWaitTime(ctx, pl.Name, fs.Name, strconv.FormatBool(req != nil), cfgCtlr.clock.Since(startWaitingTime))
   196→	}
   197→	panicking = false
   198→}
   199→
   200→func observeQueueWaitTime(ctx context.Context, priorityLevelName, flowSchemaName, execute string, waitTime time.Duration) {
   201→	metrics.ObserveWaitingDuration(ctx, priorityLevelName, flowSchemaName, execute, waitTime)
   202→	endpointsrequest.TrackAPFQueueWaitLatency(ctx, waitTime)
   203→}
   204→

arguments.content:
# API Priority and Fairness (APF) QueueSet Subsystem: Deep-Dive Analysis

## Executive Summary

The APF QueueSet subsystem implements Fair Queuing for Server Requests (FQSR), a classical fair queuing algorithm adapted for distributed API server concurrency management. It provides differentiated service through a multi-queue dispatch mechanism that ensures proportional fairness across flows while respecting per-priority-level concurrency limits. The implementation uses virtual time progress metering, shuffle sharding for load balancing, and a seat-based work model to quantify request complexity.

---

## 1. Subsystem Purpose and Boundaries

### 1.1 Purpose

The QueueSet solves the problem of fair and predictable resource allocation when multiple competing flows arrive at a Kubernetes API server with a limited concurrency budget. Rather than rejecting requests when overloaded, QueueSet queues them and dispatches in a fairness-respecting order, preventing low-frequency or critical flows from being starved by high-frequency bulk operations.

Key capabilities:
- **Flow isolation**: Each flow (e.g., one client's requests) is assigned to a queue via shuffle sharding
- **Fair dispatch**: Requests are served in virtual time order, ensuring each active flow gets proportional progress
- **Concurrency limits**: Respects both per-priority-level seat limits and queue length constraints
- **Dynamic reconfiguration**: Handles changes to queue count, queue length limits, and concurrency allocations

### 1.2 Boundaries

**Upstream dependencies (what feeds QueueSet):**
- `apf_filter.go` (FlowControl interface): Classifies requests into FlowSchemas and PriorityLevelConfigurations; calls `QueueSet.StartRequest()`
- `request/width.go` (WorkEstimate): Estimates the "width" (seats) and "additional latency" (post-execution hold time) for each request

**Downstream dependents (what QueueSet feeds):**
- The request handler (via the `Request.Finish()` callback): Controls when a request actually executes and when its concurrency is released

**Configuration sources:**
- `FlowSchema` and `PriorityLevelConfiguration` objects (from the Kubernetes API), reconciled by the `configController`

**System-level boundaries:**
- QueueSet is per-priority-level (one QueueSet per non-exempt PriorityLevelConfiguration)
- One priority level can borrow concurrency from another (orchestrated at the controller level, not within QueueSet)
- Exempt priority levels bypass queuing entirely

### 1.3 Key Assumptions

1. **Single-threaded queueset access**: All mutations are protected by a single lock; concurrent calls are serialized.
2. **Virtual time is monotonically increasing**: The progress meter R only increases (or resets at epoch boundaries).
3. **Requests are either queued or execute immediately**: No intermediate "pending" states exist.
4. **Promised decisions are independent of queueset state mutations**: A request's decision (execute vs. reject) is determined when dequeued, not when enqueued.
5. **Work estimates are conservative lower bounds**: Initial and final seat estimates reflect the maximum concurrency a request will need.

---

## 2. Component Architecture and Responsibilities

### 2.1 Core Components

#### 2.1.1 `queueSet` (staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/flowcontrol/fairqueuing/queueset/queueset.go)

**Responsibility**: Central coordinator that manages queues, virtual time, dispatch logic, and concurrency limits.

**Key fields**:
- `queues []*queue`: Array of per-flow queues; grows with DesiredNumQueues, shrinks by attrition
- `currentR SeatSeconds`: Global progress meter (virtual time) measuring seat-seconds allocated
- `lastRealTime time.Time`: Last recorded wall-clock time; used to compute virtual time increments
- `robinIndex int`: Round-robin pointer for fair queue selection during dispatch
- `totSeatsInUse, totRequestsExecuting`: Counters for active concurrency
- `clock eventclock.Interface`: Injectable clock for testing (supports event-driven time progression)
- `estimatedServiceDuration time.Duration`: Default (3ms) assumed service time; used to predict when seats will be released
- `lock sync.Mutex`: Protects all mutable state

**Critical methods**:
- `StartRequest()`: Entry point; applies shuffle sharding, enqueues, then attempts to dispatch
- `dispatchAsMuchAsPossibleLocked()`: Inner loop that dequeues and executes requests while concurrency is available
- `dispatchLocked()`: Selects the queue with minimum virtual finish time (via round-robin) and dequeues its oldest request
- `syncTimeLocked()`: Updates virtual time based on elapsed real time and active load
- `finishRequestAndDispatchAsMuchAsPossible()`: Called when a request completes; updates virtual time, releases seats, and continues dispatch

#### 2.1.2 `queue` (staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/flowcontrol/fairqueuing/queueset/types.go:91–110)

**Responsibility**: Per-flow FIFO queue that tracks requests waiting in the virtual world and the virtual finish time of the next request to dequeue.

**Key fields**:
- `requestsWaiting fifo`: FIFO list of requests not yet dispatched
- `requestsExecuting sets.Set[*request]`: Set of requests currently executing (only relevant if there is a queue for them)
- `nextDispatchR SeatSeconds`: Virtual finish time of the next (oldest waiting) request in this queue; advances by request.totalWork() after dispatch
- `seatsInUse int`: Count of concurrency currently allocated to requests from this queue that are executing
- `index int`: Position in the parent queueSet's array

**Lifecycle**:
- Created during `setConfiguration()` if DesiredNumQueues increases
- Removed by attrition when its count exceeds desired and it becomes empty (no waiting or executing requests)
- Virtual finish time is initialized to currentR when the queue becomes non-empty

#### 2.1.3 `request` (staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/flowcontrol/fairqueuing/queueset/types.go:31–89)

**Responsibility**: Wrapper for a single request, tracking its virtual-world position and real-world execution state.

**Key fields**:
- `ctx context.Context`: Request context; used for cancellation and timeout
- `queue *queue`: The queue to which this request was assigned (nil for non-queued requests)
- `arrivalR SeatSeconds`: Virtual time when the request arrived; used for anti-windup
- `arrivalTime time.Time`: Real time when the request arrived
- `startTime time.Time`: Real time when dispatch occurred (set when moved from queue to executing)
- `decision promise.WriteOnce`: Promise that holds the dispatch decision (execute vs. cancel); can be awaited without the lock
- `workEstimate completedWorkEstimate`: Pre-computed initial + final work (seat-seconds), and components
- `removeFromQueueLocked removeFromFIFOFunc`: Callback to remove from FIFO in O(1) time
- `queueNoteFn QueueNoteFn`: Optional callback invoked when request enters or leaves a queue

**Request states**:
1. **Arriving**: Request arrives via `StartRequest()`, shuffle-sharded into a queue, or immediately executed if no queues
2. **Queued**: Request waits in `queue.requestsWaiting` for dispatch
3. **Executing**: Request has been dispatched; decision is set to `decisionExecute`; request moved to `queue.requestsExecuting`
4. **Finishing**: After execution, seats are released; request removed from queue/executing set
5. **Cancelled**: Request context timed out before dispatch; decision set to `decisionCancel`

#### 2.1.4 `fifo` / `requestFIFO` (staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/flowcontrol/fairqueuing/queueset/fifo_list.go)

**Responsibility**: Efficient FIFO list backed by Go's `container/list`, with O(1) removal via stored element pointers.

**Key methods**:
- `Enqueue(req *request)`: Returns a removal function (closure) that points to the element
- `Dequeue()`: Removes and returns the front element
- `Peek()`: Returns the front element without removing
- `QueueSum()`: Aggregates `InitialSeatsSum`, `MaxSeatsSum`, `TotalWorkSum` across all waiting requests

**Why it's needed**:
Shuffle sharding selects a queue based on its waiting work (seat-seconds), so we need to efficiently track and remove from the middle of queues.

#### 2.1.5 `promise.WriteOnce` (staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/flowcontrol/fairqueuing/promise/promise.go)

**Responsibility**: Thread-safe, write-once container for the request's dispatch decision.

**Key behavior**:
- **Set()**: Can be called exactly once (enforced by `sync.Once`); closes the internal channel and stores the value
- **Get()**: Blocks until Set() is called OR the provided context is cancelled (in which case it automatically sets the context's completion value)
- Allows the QueueSet to set a decision (with lock held) and the request handler to wait on it (without lock held)

**Lifecycle**:
- Created with initial value `nil` and done-context value `decisionCancel` so that if the request context times out, the decision is automatically set to reject
- Set when the request is dequeued for dispatch (value = `decisionExecute`)

### 2.2 Supporting Components

#### 2.2.1 `eventclock.Interface` (staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/flowcontrol/fairqueuing/eventclock/)

**Purpose**: Abstraction over time that supports both real wall-clock and synthetic (test-driven) event clocks.

**Key methods**:
- `Now()`: Returns current time
- `EventAfterDuration(fn, duration)`: Schedules a callback (typically for releasing final-phase seats)

Used to support testing of virtual time progression without real delays.

#### 2.2.2 `Integrator` (staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/flowcontrol/fairqueuing/integrator.go)

**Purpose**: Tracks the time-weighted statistics of seat demand (or other gauge values) to support borrowing and fairness decisions at the priority-level controller level.

**Key fields**:
- Moments: Integral of x, x², and elapsed time (used to compute average, standard deviation, min, max)
- Used by `configController` to monitor seat demand and adjust concurrency limits

#### 2.2.3 `shufflesharding.Dealer` (staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/shufflesharding/shufflesharding.go)

**Purpose**: Implements shuffle sharding to map a hash value to a "hand" (sample) of queues; used to select which queue to enqueue to.

**Algorithm**:
- Deals a hand of `handSize` queues from the deck of `deckSize` total queues
- For each card dealt, uses sequential divisions of the hash value to uniformly distribute the choices
- Ensures low-cardinality flows (few requests) are mixed across queues, reducing head-of-line blocking

**Key methods**:
- `DealIntoHand(hashValue uint64, hand []int)`: Deals cards into the provided hand slice; shuffles based on the hash value

## 3. End-to-End Request Flow

### 3.1 High-Level Sequence

```
Request arrives
  ↓
apf_filter.Handle() classifies it into (FlowSchema, PriorityLevel, flowDistinguisher)
  ↓
Calls configController.startRequest() → QueueSet.StartRequest()
  ↓
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ If DesiredNumQueues < 1: (no queuing, only concurrency limit)
│   - Check if seats available
│   - If yes, create request, set decision to execute immediately
│   - If no, reject
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
  ↓
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ If DesiredNumQueues >= 1: (shuffle-sharded queuing)
│   1. Shuffle shard hash into a hand of queues
│   2. Select queue with min waiting work (seat-seconds)
│   3. Check concurrency and queue length limits
│   4. If both exceeded, reject
│   5. Otherwise, enqueue request into chosen queue
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
  ↓
dispatchAsMuchAsPossibleLocked()
  Loop:
    - If no requests waiting OR seats exhausted, exit
    - Find queue with min virtual finish time
    - Dequeue oldest request from that queue
    - Set its decision to execute
  ↓
Return Request handle to caller
  ↓
Caller awaits request.Finish() → decision.Get()
  (Blocks until decision is set)
  ↓
Once decision is execute:
  - Execute the callback (the actual request handler)
  - Call finishRequestAndDispatchAsMuchAsPossible()
    - Release seats
    - Update virtual time
    - Dispatch more requests if room
  ↓
Return to caller
```

### 3.2 Detailed Walkthrough with Code References

#### Phase 1: Arrival and Queue Assignment (StartRequest)

**File**: `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/flowcontrol/fairqueuing/queueset/queueset.go:308–356`

```go
func (qs *queueSet) StartRequest(ctx context.Context, workEstimate *fqrequest.WorkEstimate,
    hashValue uint64, flowDistinguisher, fsName string, descr1, descr2 interface{},
    queueNoteFn fq.QueueNoteFn) (fq.Request, bool)
```

**Steps**:
1. Acquire lock and sync virtual time: `lockAndSyncTime(ctx)` (line 309)
   - Calls `syncTimeLocked()` to advance `currentR` based on elapsed real time and active load
2. **Branch 1**: If `DesiredNumQueues < 1` (no queuing desired):
   - Check if seats available via `canAccommodateSeatsLocked()` (line 317)
   - If not, reject with metrics (line 320-321)
   - If yes, call `dispatchSansQueueLocked()` to create an immediately-executing request (line 324)
   - Return request with `idle=false` (line 325)

3. **Branch 2**: If `DesiredNumQueues >= 1` (queuing enabled):
   - Call `shuffleShardAndRejectOrEnqueueLocked()` (line 334)
   - This returns a request or nil (if rejected)
   - If nil (rejected), reject with metrics (line 339-340)

4. Call `dispatchAsMuchAsPossibleLocked()` to attempt immediate dispatch (line 353)
5. Return the request

#### Phase 2: Shuffle Sharding and Enqueue (shuffleShardAndRejectOrEnqueueLocked)

**File**: `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/flowcontrol/fairqueuing/queueset/queueset.go:552–579`

```go
func (qs *queueSet) shuffleShardAndRejectOrEnqueueLocked(ctx context.Context,
    workEstimate *fqrequest.WorkEstimate, hashValue uint64, ...) *request
```

**Steps**:
1. Call `shuffleShardLocked(hashValue, ...)` (line 554):
   - Use the dealer to generate a hand of `handSize` queue indices (line 587)
   - Iterate through the hand and select the queue with minimum waiting work (line 593-606)
   - Ties are broken by order (first in hand wins)

2. Create a `request` struct with:
   - Current virtual time (`arrivalR = qs.currentR`)
   - Current real time (`arrivalTime = qs.clock.Now()`)
   - A promise for the dispatch decision (line 565)
   - Pre-computed work estimate (line 572)

3. Call `rejectOrEnqueueToBoundLocked()` (line 574):
   - If `totSeatsInUse >= ConcurrencyLimit AND curQueueLength >= QueueLengthLimit`, reject
   - Otherwise, enqueue the request into the queue (line 627)
     - Initialize the queue's `nextDispatchR = currentR` if it was empty (line 637)
     - Add request to `requestsWaiting` FIFO (line 643)
     - Update metrics and counters (line 644-650)

#### Phase 3: Dispatch Loop (dispatchAsMuchAsPossibleLocked)

**File**: `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/flowcontrol/fairqueuing/queueset/queueset.go:653–657`

```go
func (qs *queueSet) dispatchAsMuchAsPossibleLocked() {
    for qs.totRequestsWaiting != 0 && qs.totSeatsInUse < qs.dCfg.ConcurrencyLimit &&
        qs.dispatchLocked() {
    }
}
```

**Termination conditions**:
- No requests waiting
- No seats available
- `dispatchLocked()` returns false (queue selection failed or request cannot be accommodated)

#### Phase 4: Fair Queue Selection and Dequeue (dispatchLocked + findDispatchQueueToBoundLocked)

**File**: `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/flowcontrol/fairqueuing/queueset/queueset.go:695–743`

```go
func (qs *queueSet) dispatchLocked() bool {
    queue, request := qs.findDispatchQueueToBoundLocked()
    ...
}

func (qs *queueSet) findDispatchQueueToBoundLocked() (*queue, *request) {
    minVirtualFinish := MaxSeatSeconds
    for range qs.queues {
        qs.robinIndex = (qs.robinIndex + 1) % nq  // Round-robin
        queue := qs.queues[qs.robinIndex]
        oldestWaiting, _ := queue.requestsWaiting.Peek()
        if oldestWaiting != nil {
            currentVirtualFinish := queue.nextDispatchR + oldestWaiting.totalWork()
            if currentVirtualFinish < minVirtualFinish {
                minVirtualFinish = currentVirtualFinish
                minQueue = queue
                minIndex = qs.robinIndex
            }
        }
    }
    ...
}
```

**Algorithm** (Fair Queuing):
1. Round-robin iteration through queues (line 786)
2. For each non-empty queue, compute the virtual finish time of its oldest request:
   - `virtualFinish = queue.nextDispatchR + request.totalWork()`
   - `queue.nextDispatchR` is the "virtual start time" for the next request in this queue
   - `request.totalWork()` is the work (seat-seconds) for this request

3. Select the queue with minimum virtual finish time (line 797)
4. Tie-breaking: First queue in round-robin order wins (implicit in loop order)

**Dispatch**:
1. Dequeue the oldest request from the selected queue (line 822)
2. Set `request.decision` to `decisionExecute` via its promise (line 710)
3. Move the request from `requestsWaiting` to `requestsExecuting` (line 722)
4. Update the queue's `nextDispatchR` by adding the request's total work (line 741)
5. Apply anti-windup hack (line 952): Ensure `nextDispatchR >= oldestWaiting.arrivalR` to prevent virtual time from exceeding arrival time

#### Phase 5: Request Execution and Completion (Finish)

**File**: `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/flowcontrol/fairqueuing/queueset/queueset.go:381–395`

```go
func (req *request) Finish(execFn func()) bool {
    exec, idle := req.wait()
    if !exec {
        return idle
    }
    defer func() {
        idle = req.qs.finishRequestAndDispatchAsMuchAsPossible(req)
    }()
    execFn()
    return idle
}
```

**Steps**:
1. Call `req.wait()` to obtain the dispatch decision (line 382)
   - Blocks on `decision.Get()` until a decision is available
   - If context is cancelled, decision is automatically set to `decisionCancel`
   - If decision is `decisionCancel`, return false (request is rejected)

2. If decision is `decisionExecute`, execute the callback (line 391)

3. Call `finishRequestAndDispatchAsMuchAsPossible()` (line 388):
   - Acquire lock, sync time (line 852)
   - Call `finishRequestLocked(req)` (line 855)
     - Decrement `totRequestsExecuting` (line 865)
     - Calculate actual service duration (line 869)
     - Update queue's `nextDispatchR` by subtracting the delta between estimated and actual service time (line 938)
     - Apply anti-windup again (line 939)
     - Release seats (line 877) or schedule delayed release for additional latency phase (line 915-929)
     - Remove queue if it's become empty and we have excess queues (line 966-984)
   - Call `dispatchAsMuchAsPossibleLocked()` again (line 856)
   - Return idle status

### 3.3 Virtual Time Mechanics

#### Virtual Time Synchronization (syncTimeLocked)

**File**: `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/flowcontrol/fairqueuing/queueset/queueset.go:461–479`

```go
func (qs *queueSet) syncTimeLocked(ctx context.Context) {
    realNow := qs.clock.Now()
    timeSinceLast := realNow.Sub(qs.lastRealTime)
    qs.lastRealTime = realNow
    prevR := qs.currentR
    incrR := fqrequest.SeatsTimesDuration(qs.getVirtualTimeRatioLocked(), timeSinceLast)
    qs.currentR = prevR + incrR
    ...
}
```

**Key insight**: Virtual time advances proportionally to the "virtual time ratio":

```
virtual time ratio = min(total seat requests, ConcurrencyLimit) / number of active queues
```

This ensures that:
- If 10 queues are active and concurrency limit is 100 seats, each queue gets 10 seat-seconds per real second
- If only 1 queue is active, it gets the full 100 seat-seconds per real second
- If queues request 200 seats but concurrency limit is 100, queues advance at 100 seat-seconds per real second

#### Virtual Time Overflow Mitigation (Epoch Advancement)

**File**: `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/flowcontrol/fairqueuing/queueset/queueset.go:481–522`

```go
const rDecrement = fqrequest.MaxSeatSeconds / 2
const highR = rDecrement + rDecrement/2

func (qs *queueSet) advanceEpoch(ctx context.Context, now time.Time, incrR fqrequest.SeatSeconds) {
    oldR := qs.currentR
    qs.currentR -= rDecrement
    // Decrement all queue.nextDispatchR and request.arrivalR by rDecrement
    ...
}
```

**Why it's needed**:
- `SeatSeconds` is a float64, which can overflow after very long running (e.g., years of operation)
- When `currentR` exceeds `highR` (MaxSeatSeconds × 0.75), the system "advances the epoch" by subtracting `MaxSeatSeconds × 0.5` from all progress meters
- This is equivalent to resetting the coordinate system while preserving relative ordering

## 4. Fairness Guarantees and Tradeoffs

### 4.1 Fairness Model

The QueueSet provides **work-conserving fair queuing** with the following properties:

1. **Weighted Fairness**: Each queue receives service proportional to its share of active demand
   - Fair share = `ConcurrencyLimit / num_active_queues`
   - A queue with more requests waiting gets no preferential treatment; it's enqueued in the same queues as low-demand flows

2. **Virtual Time Ordering**: Dispatch is strictly ordered by virtual finish time
   - If two queues are both active and have the same concurrency limit share, the queue with the smaller virtual finish time is served first
   - This prevents a queue from "jumping ahead" by having broader requests

3. **Shuffle Sharding Balancing**: Requests within the same flow are scattered across multiple queues
   - A single bursty flow occupies only `handSize` queues instead of monopolizing one
   - This reduces head-of-line blocking (slow requests don't block the entire flow)

### 4.2 Known Limitations and Tradeoffs

#### 4.2.1 Single Global Lock

**Tradeoff**: All mutable state is protected by a single `sync.Mutex`.

**Impact**:
- Ensures correctness and consistency (no race conditions between queue selection and dispatch)
- Can become a bottleneck if `StartRequest()`, `Finish()`, and dispatch loops contend heavily
- Latency of virtual time updates is bounded by lock-holding time

**Mitigation**: Lock-holding periods are kept small; expensive operations (like metrics recording) happen outside the lock.

#### 4.2.2 Shuffle Sharding Overhead

**Tradeoff**: Shuffle sharding requires `Dealer.Deal()` to generate a hand of queue indices via modular arithmetic.

**Cost**: O(handSize) bit operations per enqueue (negligible; typically handSize ≤ 15).

**Benefit**: Prevents a single bursty flow from monopolizing one queue.

#### 4.2.3 Virtual Time Estimation Errors

**Tradeoff**: Service time is estimated via `estimatedServiceDuration` (default 3ms); actual times may vary.

**Impact**:
- If actual service time < estimate, queue's `nextDispatchR` is incremented (punishing the queue for being faster)
- If actual service time > estimate, queue's `nextDispatchR` is decremented (rewarding the queue for being slower)
- This dampens oscillations but introduces bias if estimates are consistently wrong

**Mitigation**: The estimate is conservative (3ms is a typical list operation); most requests are faster, so queues are rarely punished. Configurable via code, not via API objects.

#### 4.2.4 Queue Removal by Attrition

**Tradeoff**: Reducing `DesiredNumQueues` doesn't immediately remove excess queues; they drain by attrition (when they become empty).

**Impact**:
- If a queue has long-lived requests, the queue persists even after desired count drops
- During configuration updates, total queue count may temporarily exceed desired count

**Benefit**: Avoids disrupting in-flight requests; maintains isolation guarantees.

#### 4.2.5 Seat-Based Work Model Limitations

**Tradeoff**: Work is modeled as "seats × duration"; actual CPU, memory, and I/O costs are not distinguished.

**Impact**:
- A 100-seat request that does I/O-only work monopolizes concurrency but uses little CPU
- A 1-seat request with heavy CPU computation is starved
- Seat allocation is per-priority-level, so no cross-priority admission decisions are made here

**Mitigation**: Work estimation is delegable (via `workEstimator` parameter); custom estimators can cap seats per request type (e.g., watch operations vs. list operations).

#### 4.2.6 No Preemption

**Tradeoff**: Once a request is executing, it cannot be preempted; dispatch is only at the boundary (when a request calls `Finish()`).

**Impact**:
- A low-priority request that is currently executing blocks dispatch of high-priority requests
- Wait times are bounded by the longest executing request

**Rationale**: Preemption in HTTP request processing is complex (state cleanup, partial response handling); not feasible for a general-purpose API server.

### 4.3 Fairness in Practice

**Example scenario**: Two flows A and B, each with a queue, and ConcurrencyLimit = 10 seats.

1. Flow A sends 5 requests, each requiring 4 seats (total 20 seats demanded)
2. Flow B sends 1 request requiring 2 seats
3. Initial dispatch:
   - Both flows are active, so virtual time ratio = min(20+2, 10) / 2 = 5 seat-seconds per real second per queue
   - If all 5 of A's requests start, they occupy 20 seats > 10 limit, so only 2.5 on average can run
   - Request 1 of A gets dispatched, occupies 4 seats (6 remain)
   - Request 1 of B gets dispatched, occupies 2 seats (4 remain)
   - Request 2 of A gets dispatched, occupies 4 seats (0 remain)
   - Dispatch loop exits: no more seats

4. Virtual time advances as requests execute:
   - If A's request 1 takes 1 second at 4 seats, its virtual finish time = arrivalR + 4 * 1 = arrivalR + 4
   - If B's request takes 1 second at 2 seats, its virtual finish time = arrivalR + 2 * 1 = arrivalR + 2
   - B's request finishes first (lower virtual finish time)
   - When B's request completes, B has released 2 seats; A's queue 3 can now be dispatched

---

## 5. Failure Modes and Design Limits

### 5.1 Virtual Time Overflow

**Condition**: `currentR` exceeds `MaxSeatSeconds` (float64 ~= 9.22e18 seat-seconds)

**Effect**: Epoch advancement is triggered, subtracting `rDecrement` from all progress meters

**Manifestation**: Logs show "Advancing epoch" message (line 495)

**Limits**: With ConcurrencyLimit = 5000 and continuous load, overflow occurs after ~60 million years (not a practical concern)

### 5.2 Queue Underflow

**Condition**: During epoch advancement, `queue.nextDispatchR` or `request.arrivalR` would underflow (become larger after subtraction)

**Effect**: Logged as error: "Underflow" (line 507-508)

**Causes**: Rare; indicates a logic error in virtual time management (e.g., nextDispatchR not properly advanced after dispatch)

### 5.3 Request Dispatch Before Arrival

**Condition**: `queue.nextDispatchR < request.arrivalR` after dequeue

**Effect**: Logged as error: "dispatch before arrival" (line 840)

**Rationale**: This is prevented by the anti-windup hack (boundNextDispatchLocked), which ensures nextDispatchR >= arrivalR

### 5.4 Concurrency Limit Exceeded

**Condition**: `totSeatsInUse + request.MaxSeats() > ConcurrencyLimit` when a request is dispatched

**Detection**: During dispatch, `canAccommodateSeatsLocked()` is checked; should prevent dispatch if violated

**Fallback**: Requests with `MaxSeats > ConcurrencyLimit` can only proceed when all other requests are idle (line 757-760)

### 5.5 Promise Decision Not Set

**Condition**: A request's decision is never set (neither execute nor cancel)

**Detection**: `req.wait()` blocks indefinitely on `decision.Get()`; context timeout sets decision to cancel

**Causes**: Logic error in dispatch loop (should not happen if dispatchLocked() properly calls `decision.Set()`)

### 5.6 Queue Length Limit Exceeded

**Condition**: More requests are enqueued than `QueueLengthLimit * DesiredNumQueues`

**Detection**: Checked in `rejectOrEnqueueToBoundLocked()` (line 623)

**Fallback**: Request is rejected with "queue-full" reason

---

## 6. Configuration and Reconfiguration

### 6.1 Two-Phase Creation

The factory pattern enables validation before committing resources:

```go
// Phase 1: Validate queuing config (expensive only if invalid)
completer, err := factory.BeginConstruction(queuingConfig)
if err != nil {
    return err  // Reject before allocating queues
}

// Phase 2: Allocate dispatching capacity
qs := completer.Complete(dispatchingConfig)
```

**Benefit**: Errors in one part of config (e.g., invalid HandSize) are caught before the other part is committed.

### 6.2 Reconfiguration via Configuration Change

**File**: `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/flowcontrol/fairqueuing/queueset/queueset.go:239–287`

```go
func (qs *queueSet) BeginConfigChange(qCfg fq.QueuingConfig) (fq.QueueSetCompleter, error)
func (qs *queueSet) setConfiguration(ctx context.Context, qCfg fq.QueuingConfig,
    dealer *shufflesharding.Dealer, dCfg fq.DispatchingConfig)
```

**Behavior**:
- If `DesiredNumQueues` increases, new queues are immediately created (line 264-265)
- If `DesiredNumQueues` decreases (or becomes zero), queues are removed by attrition (no immediate removal)
- If `DesiredNumQueues` becomes zero, queuing is disabled; concurrency limit is the only constraint
- If `DesiredNumQueues` becomes negative, the queue set is "exempt" from all limits (used for system-critical requests)

**Metrics update**: `reqsGaugePair.RequestsWaiting.SetDenominator()` and related calls (line 282-284) adjust prometheus denominator for ratio gauges.

### 6.3 Stable Reconfigurations

**Invariant**: Configurations that don't change `DesiredNumQueues` do not alter the queue structure.

Example: Increasing `ConcurrencyLimit` only updates `dCfg.ConcurrencyDenominator` and may trigger borrowing at the controller level; no queue reorganization.
